top_stories.title: Omada Launches Omada Identity Cloud Private for Regulated Enterprises and Government Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched "Omada Identity Cloud Private," a new deployment option that runs the full Identity Cloud platform within the customer's own tenant. The offering is designed for regulated enterprises and government organizations that require specific data boundary controls and audit compliance.

top_stories.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
top_stories.summary: Attackers are abusing Windows Phone Link to steal SMS messages and bypass two-factor authentication using CloudZ RAT and a new plugin called Pheno. The attack hijacks the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones to intercept OTP codes delivered via SMS, undermining SMS-based 2FA.

top_stories.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits quickly emerging afterward. A researcher claims zero-day exploitation of the flaw had already been occurring for at least a month prior to public disclosure.

top_stories.title: Your coworker might be selling company logins, and thinks it’s fine
top_stories.summary: A Cifas study of 2,000 UK employees at large companies found that selling login credentials and other forms of workplace fraud are increasingly viewed as justifiable behavior. The research highlights a growing trend of employees monetizing company access credentials, with the behavior observed across industries including IT and telecommunications, and at all seniority levels.

top_stories.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
top_stories.summary: Gartner has published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, identifying AI agents as an emerging governance challenge for identity security teams. The report states that enterprise AI agent adoption is outpacing the maturity of governance policy controls, raising access management concerns for IAM professionals.

news.title: Ivanti warns of new EPMM flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
news.summary: Ivanti has disclosed a high-severity remote code execution vulnerability in its Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) product that is being actively exploited as a zero-day. Ivanti is urging customers to apply patches immediately to address the flaw.

news.title: Facial recognition arrives at the gates of Disney’s magic kingdom
news.summary: Disney has deployed facial recognition technology at select entrance lanes at Disneyland Park and Disney California Adventure Park to streamline re-entry and prevent ticket fraud. The system captures guest images linked to tickets or passes, converting biometric data into numerical values for comparison at entry points.

news.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
news.summary: Researchers have disclosed PCPJack, a credential theft framework that targets exposed cloud infrastructure by exploiting five CVEs to spread across systems. The toolset harvests credentials from cloud, container, developer, productivity, and financial services before exfiltrating data through attacker-controlled infrastructure.

news.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
news.summary: Researchers disclosed details of an attack using CloudZ RAT and a previously undocumented plugin called "Pheno" that exploited Windows Phone Link to steal credentials and one-time passwords (OTPs). The malware was specifically designed to intercept OTPs, targeting multi-factor authentication mechanisms used to protect identity verification.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server is now generally available
news.summary: AWS has announced the general availability of the AWS MCP Server, a managed remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides AI agents and coding assistants with authenticated access to AWS services. The product is part of AWS's Agent Toolkit and focuses on enabling secure, authenticated connections between AI tools and AWS infrastructure.

news.title: Keeper Security Research Reveals 89% of IT Leaders Struggle to Manage Growing Identity Footprint Amid AI Expansion
news.summary: Keeper Security released a global study of 3,200 cybersecurity decision-makers finding that 89% of IT leaders struggle to manage growing identity footprints amid AI expansion. The research found 96% of respondents report that disconnected identity tools are creating exploitable security gaps, with AI adoption identified as a key driver of identity sprawl.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing someone with administrative privileges to extract stored credentials. The vulnerability highlights risks for enterprise environments where credential theft could enable further lateral movement or privilege escalation.

news.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
news.summary: The article examines persistent OAuth tokens created by third-party app integrations with Google and Microsoft as a security vulnerability exploited by attackers. These tokens have no expiration dates, bypass MFA and perimeter controls, and are largely unmonitored in most organizations.

news.title: Microsoft Details Phishing Campaign Targeting 35,000 Users Across 26 Countries
news.summary: Microsoft disclosed a large-scale credential theft and authentication token hijacking campaign observed between April 14-16, 2026, targeting over 35,000 users across 13,000+ organizations in 26 countries. The multi-stage attack used code of conduct-themed lures combined with legitimate email services to redirect victims to attacker-controlled domains for token theft.

news.title: Modernize your workflows: Amazon WorkSpaces now gives AI agents their own desktop (preview)
news.summary: Amazon WorkSpaces has launched a preview feature allowing AI agents to operate legacy desktop applications using IAM authentication, MCP support, and computer vision. The feature uses IAM authentication as part of the security framework enabling AI agents to interact with desktop environments without requiring API integration or application modernization.

news.title: Secure AI agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity on Amazon ECS
news.summary: Amazon has launched Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity, a standalone service designed to secure how AI agents authenticate and access external services. The service is designed to work across AWS compute platforms including Amazon ECS and Amazon EKS, addressing identity and authorization challenges for agentic AI workloads.

news.title: Five ways to use Kiro and Amazon Q to strengthen your security posture
news.summary: The article describes five use cases for combining Kiro and Amazon Q Developer to address security issues including AWS IAM policy violations. The content focuses primarily on AWS developer tooling and general security posture, with IAM appearing as one of several incidental topics rather than the central focus.

news.title: What are managed identities in SQL Server 2025? A complete guide
news.summary: SQL Server 2025 introduces managed identities that eliminate passwords and enable Microsoft Entra authentication for Azure resources. The feature allows SQL Server instances to authenticate to Azure services without storing credentials, using system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity types.

news.title: Regula Warns Digital Identity Has a Human Problem
news.summary: Regula has issued a warning that digital identity and authentication systems have a fundamental human problem, arguing that financial services identity infrastructure is inadequate beyond the initial authentication moment. The company contends that current systems rely too heavily on a single point-in-time identity verification rather than continuous authentication.

news.title: AWS IAM now provides higher maximum quotas for roles, role trust policies, instance profiles, managed policies, and identity providers
news.summary: AWS IAM has increased maximum quotas for six resources, including doubling customer managed policies per account from 5,000 to 10,000 and instance profiles per account from 5,000 to 10,000. Managed policies per role limits were also increased, along with other IAM resource quotas affecting roles, role trust policies, and identity providers.

news.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
news.summary: Progress Software has released security patches for MOVEit Automation addressing two vulnerabilities, including a critical authentication bypass flaw. The vulnerability affects MOVEit Automation (formerly Central), a server-based managed file transfer solution used in enterprise environments.

news.title: Workload Identity Federation
news.summary: Anthropic has introduced Workload Identity Federation (WIF) for the Claude API, allowing workloads to authenticate using short-lived identity tokens from external identity providers instead of long-lived static API keys. The feature leverages OpenID Connect (OIDC) to enable federated authentication for machine-to-machine access to the Claude API.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to participating in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying solely on router flaws to silently siphon authentication tokens at scale.
